_comment: 10 originalnih, istorijski tačnih blog postova o cigarama. Sažeci na index showcase-u, pun tekst na /blog stranici.

posts.excerptsr: U novembru 1492. dvojica Kolumbovih mornara, Rodrigo de Jerez i Luis de Torres, postaju prvi Evropljani koji su videli ljude kako udišu dim sušenog duvana. Rodrigo će kasnije zbog te navike provesti tri godine u zatvoru španske inkvizicije.
posts.excerpten: In November 1492 two of Columbus's sailors, Rodrigo de Jerez and Luis de Torres, became the first Europeans to witness people inhaling the smoke of dried tobacco. Rodrigo would later spend three years in the Spanish Inquisition's prisons because of the habit.
posts.bodysr:
Kada je Kristofor Kolumbo 28. oktobra 1492. iskrcao posadu na obale Kube, među prvim stvarima koje su mornari primetili bila je čudna navika domorodaca: palili su uvijene listove biljke koju su zvali *cohiba*, držali ih u ustima i ispuštali dim. Kolumbo je u dnevniku zapisao: *„Indijanci nose šibe goruće u ustima i pijuckaju duvan."*

Dvojica njegovih ljudi, Rodrigo de Jerez i Luis de Torres (jevrejski prevodilac koji je govorio hebrejski, aramejski i arapski), uputili su se kopnom u istraživanje. Vratili su se sa pričom o domorocima koji *„piju dim"*. Rodrigo je doneo navikom kući u Ayamonte u Andaluziji. Komšije su ga, videvši dim kako mu izlazi iz nosa, optužile da ga *„opseda đavo"*. Inkvizicija ga je zatvorila na sedam godina. Kada je izašao, duvan je već bio rasprostranjen po Evropi.

Ime *„cohiba"* preživelo je do danas — Fidel Castro je 1966. tako nazvao kubansku državnu marku koja se i dalje smatra najekskluzivnijom cigarom na svetu.
posts.bodyen:
When Christopher Columbus landed his crew on Cuba's coast on October 28, 1492, one of the first things the sailors noticed was a strange custom of the locals: they would burn rolled leaves of a plant they called *cohiba*, hold them in their mouths and exhale smoke. Columbus wrote in his diary: *"The Indians carry burning sticks in their mouths and sip tobacco."*

Two of his men, Rodrigo de Jerez and Luis de Torres (a Jewish interpreter who spoke Hebrew, Aramaic and Arabic), went inland to explore. They returned with stories of natives who *"drink smoke."* Rodrigo brought the habit home to Ayamonte in Andalusia. His neighbours, seeing smoke coming out of his nose, accused him of being *"possessed by the devil."* The Inquisition imprisoned him for seven years. By the time he was released, tobacco was already spreading across Europe.

The name *cohiba* survived to this day — in 1966 Fidel Castro gave it to the Cuban state brand still considered the most exclusive cigar in the world.

posts.titlesr: Cohiba — cigara rezervisana samo za Fidela
posts.titleen: Cohiba — the cigar reserved only for Fidel
posts.excerptsr: Sredinom 1960-ih, telohranitelj Fidela Castra Bienvenido „Chicho" Pérez pušio je cigaru koja je toliko impresionirala lidera da je zatražio njenog tvorca. Tako je nastala Cohiba — marka koja se godinama nije mogla kupiti ni za novac.
posts.excerpten: In the mid-1960s, Fidel Castro's bodyguard Bienvenido "Chicho" Pérez was smoking a cigar so impressive the leader asked for its maker. That's how Cohiba was born — a brand that for years couldn't be bought for any money.
posts.bodysr:
Eduardo Rivera Irizarri, mladi torcedor iz Habane, motao je cigare za sebe i prijatelje. Jednog dana 1966. njegov prijatelj Chicho Pérez, telohranitelj predsednika Castra, ponudio mu je cigaru iz lične rezerve. Castro je povukao dim, zastao i upitao: *„Odakle ti ovo?"*

Narednog dana Rivera je premešten u tajnu fabriku *El Laguito*, vilu u zapadnoj Habani gde su lidere režima motale samo izabrane majstorice. Cohiba je do 1982. bila *„cigara koja ne postoji"* — koristila se isključivo kao diplomatski poklon stranim šefovima država. Tek na Svetskom kupu fudbala u Španiji 1982. marka je predstavljena javnosti.

Danas *Cohiba Behike 56* važi za jednu od najskupljih komercijalnih cigara na svetu — kutija od 25 komada košta preko 7000 evra.
posts.bodyen:
Eduardo Rivera Irizarri, a young torcedor from Havana, used to roll cigars for himself and friends. One day in 1966 his friend Chicho Pérez, bodyguard to President Castro, offered him a cigar from his personal stash. Castro took a draw, paused, and asked: *"Where did you get this?"*

The next day Rivera was moved to a secret factory, *El Laguito*, a villa in western Havana where only chosen master rollers worked for the regime's leaders. Until 1982, Cohiba was *"the cigar that didn't exist"* — used solely as a diplomatic gift to foreign heads of state. Only at the 1982 FIFA World Cup in Spain was the brand introduced to the public.

Today *Cohiba Behike 56* is considered one of the world's most expensive commercial cigars — a box of 25 sells for over €7,000.

posts.titlesr: Vinston Čerčil i Romeo y Julieta
posts.titleen: Winston Churchill and Romeo y Julieta
posts.excerptsr: Britanski premijer pušio je oko deset cigara dnevno tokom najtežih dana Drugog svetskog rata. Romeo y Julieta je 1948. lansirala vitolu *Churchill* — 7 inča dugu, 47 ringa — koja je do danas standard cele jedne klase cigara.
posts.excerpten: The British Prime Minister smoked around ten cigars a day during the darkest days of WWII. In 1948 Romeo y Julieta launched the *Churchill* vitola — 7 inches long, 47 ring — which to this day defines an entire class of cigars.
posts.bodysr:
Vinston Čerčil je sa cigarama upoznat kao mladi oficir 1895. godine, na izveštačkoj misiji u Kubi tokom Desetogodišnjeg rata. Vratio se kući sa navikom koja će ga pratiti sledećih 70 godina. Procena je da je popušio preko 250.000 cigara tokom života.

Njegov omiljeni brend bio je *Romeo y Julieta*, kubanska kuća osnovana 1875. Vlasnik fabrike, Pepín Rodríguez Fernández, lično je 1948. lansirao novu vitolu i nazvao je *Churchill* u čast premijera koji je upravo bio poražen na izborima ali ostao moralna ikona slobodnog sveta.

Format 7×47 brzo je postao standard. Danas svaka veća kuća ima svoju *Churchill* vitolu. Britanska kraljevska porodica i dalje na zvanične događaje poklanja kutije Romeo y Julieta — gestu poštovanja prema čoveku koji je *„popušio rat".*
posts.bodyen:
Winston Churchill was introduced to cigars as a young officer in 1895, on a reporting mission to Cuba during the Ten Years' War. He came home with a habit that stayed with him for the next 70 years. He is estimated to have smoked over 250,000 cigars in his lifetime.

His favourite brand was *Romeo y Julieta*, a Cuban house founded in 1875. The factory's owner, Pepín Rodríguez Fernández, personally launched a new vitola in 1948 and named it *Churchill* in honour of the Prime Minister who had just been defeated at the polls but remained a moral icon of the free world.

The 7×47 format quickly became a standard. Today every major house has its own *Churchill* vitola. The British royal family still gifts Romeo y Julieta boxes at state events — a gesture of respect to the man who *"smoked his way through the war."*

posts.titlesr: Embargo i veliko bekstvo kubanskih dinastija
posts.titleen: The embargo and the great escape of Cuban dynasties
posts.excerptsr: Pre nego što je 7. februara 1962. potpisao embargo, predsednik Kenedi je svom press-sekretaru Pierreu Salingeru naredio da kupi 1.200 H. Upmann Petit Coronas. Slika tog dana definisala je vek ispred sebe.
posts.excerpten: Before signing the embargo on February 7, 1962, President Kennedy instructed his press secretary Pierre Salinger to buy 1,200 H. Upmann Petit Coronas. The picture of that day defined the century ahead.
posts.bodysr:
Predsednik Džon F. Kenedi 6. februara 1962. zvao je Pierrea Salingera u Bilu kuću. *„Pierre, treba mi 1.000 H. Upmann Petit Coronas pre osam ujutro."* Salinger je obišao sve duvandžije u Vašingtonu i sledećeg jutra doneo 1.200 komada. Tek tada je Kenedi potpisao Proklamaciju 3447 kojom je uveden trgovinski embargo Kubi — koji važi i danas.

Kubanske dinastije fabrika nisu čekale. Porodice *Padrón*, *Plasencia*, *Fuente*, *García* i *Toraño* prebegle su u Dominikansku Republiku, Honduras i Nikaragvu i ponele sa sobom semenje, znanje i tradiciju. Tako su nastale današnje *Padrón 1964 Anniversary*, *Arturo Fuente Opus X*, *Plasencia Alma Fuerte* — cigare koje danas u slepim degustacijama često nadvise kubanske rivale.

Ironija je da je upravo embargo, dizajniran da uguši kubansku ekonomiju, otvorio vrata renesansi karipske cigare van ostrva.
posts.bodyen:
On February 6, 1962, President John F. Kennedy called Pierre Salinger into the White House. *"Pierre, I need 1,000 H. Upmann Petit Coronas before eight in the morning."* Salinger drove around every tobacconist in Washington and brought 1,200 the next morning. Only then did Kennedy sign Proclamation 3447, instituting the Cuban trade embargo — still in force today.

The Cuban factory dynasties didn't wait. The *Padrón*, *Plasencia*, *Fuente*, *García* and *Toraño* families fled to the Dominican Republic, Honduras and Nicaragua, taking seeds, know-how and tradition with them. From this exodus came today's *Padrón 1964 Anniversary*, *Arturo Fuente Opus X*, *Plasencia Alma Fuerte* — cigars that today often outscore their Cuban rivals in blind tastings.

The irony is that the embargo, designed to choke Cuba's economy, opened the door to the renaissance of Caribbean cigars off the island.

posts.titlesr: Torcedor — zanat koji se uči pet godina
posts.titleen: The torcedor — a craft that takes five years to learn
posts.excerptsr: U fabrici *Partagás* u Habani, osnovanoj 1845, *lectores* (čitači) i danas glasno čitaju romane majstorima dok motaju cigare. Tako je grupa torcedora 1865. dobila ime *Montecristo* — po Dimasovom romanu kojim su bili opčinjeni.
posts.excerpten: In Havana's *Partagás* factory, founded in 1845, *lectores* (readers) still read novels aloud to the masters while they roll cigars. That's how a group of torcedores got the name *Montecristo* in 1865 — after the Dumas novel they were obsessed with.
posts.bodysr:
Da bi postao *torcedor* prvog ranga (*galera*) u kubanskoj fabrici, čovek mora da prođe minimum pet godina obuke. Učenje počinje od najjeftinije vitole *Coronas* (5,5 × 42), pa se napreduje ka kompleksnim figuradama — *Pyramides*, *Belicoso*, *Salomones*. Najzahtevniju, *Diademas* dužine preko 23 cm, mota svega nekoliko ljudi na svetu.

Tradicija *lectora* (čitača) datira iz 1865. U fabrici *El Figaro* radnici su skupljali novac da plate čoveka koji bi im čitao novine i romane dok su radili. Najpopularniji autor bio je Aleksandar Dima — *Grof Monte Kristo* je pročitan toliko puta da su radnici sami počeli da daju cigarama imena likova: *Montecristo* (samo ime), *Edmondo Dantes*, *Mercedes*. Kuća *Montecristo* je zvanično osnovana 1935. godine.

Danas *lectores* čitaju i Garcíu Márqueza, vesti i sportske rezultate. Glas iznad fabrike, u tišini ruku koje motaju, deo je zaštićene UNESCO baštine.
posts.bodyen:
To become a first-rank *torcedor* (*galera*) in a Cuban factory, one must complete at least five years of training. Learning starts with the simplest *Coronas* (5.5 × 42) vitola, then progresses to complex figurados — *Pyramides*, *Belicoso*, *Salomones*. The most demanding, *Diademas* over 23 cm long, are rolled by only a handful of people in the world.

The tradition of the *lector* (reader) dates from 1865. In the *El Figaro* factory, workers pooled money to pay someone to read newspapers and novels to them as they worked. The most popular author was Alexandre Dumas — *The Count of Monte Cristo* was read so many times that workers began naming cigars after the characters: *Montecristo* (the name itself), *Edmondo Dantes*, *Mercedes*. The *Montecristo* house was officially founded in 1935.

Today *lectores* read García Márquez, the news, and sports scores. The voice above the factory, in the silence of rolling hands, is part of UNESCO's protected heritage.

posts.titleen: The humidor — the science of 70 percent
posts.excerptsr: Britanski stolar Edward Pinder 1895. patentirao je prvi komercijalni *cabinet humidor* za londonski klub St. James's. Magični broj — 70 odsto relativne vlažnosti — nije izabran slučajno: to je tačka na kojoj duvan ne pleše, ne sahne i ne raste.
posts.excerpten: British carpenter Edward Pinder patented the first commercial *cabinet humidor* in 1895 for London's St. James's Club. The magic number — 70 percent relative humidity — wasn't picked at random: it's the point at which tobacco doesn't mould, dry, or grow.
posts.bodysr:
Idealan humidor održava dva broja: **70% relativnu vlažnost** i **18-21°C**. Ispod 65% cigara puca i gubi ulje. Iznad 75% započinju mikrobiološke procese — tobacco beetle (*Lasioderma serricorne*) i plesni. Zato je drvo unutrašnjosti uvek **španski kedar** (*Cedrela odorata*) — odbija insekte aromatičnim uljima i upija višak vlage.

Danas se koriste tri tehnologije: klasične *humidifier solution* gel-jedinice, dvosmerni *Boveda* paketi (precizno kalibrisani 65/69/72/75% RH) i elektronski humidori sa Peltier hlađenjem. Najprestižniji su japanski *Hitachi* i italijanski *Adorini Deluxe* — kombinacija ručno tapaciranog kedrа i digitalnog higrometra.

Kolekcionarski humidori dostižu cene preko 100.000 dolara. *Daniel Marshall Royal Humidor* prodat je 2018. za 185.000 dolara — kutija od burl javorovog korena, ojačana titanom, sa biblioteci od 250 cigara.
posts.bodyen:
An ideal humidor maintains two numbers: **70% relative humidity** and **18-21°C**. Below 65%, the cigar cracks and loses oils. Above 75%, microbiological processes start — tobacco beetle (*Lasioderma serricorne*) and mould. That's why the inner wood is always **Spanish cedar** (*Cedrela odorata*) — its aromatic oils repel insects and absorb excess humidity.

Three technologies are used today: classical *humidifier solution* gel units, two-way *Boveda* packs (precisely calibrated to 65/69/72/75% RH), and electronic humidors with Peltier cooling. The most prestigious are Japan's *Hitachi* and Italy's *Adorini Deluxe* — hand-lined cedar paired with a digital hygrometer.

Collector humidors reach prices over $100,000. The *Daniel Marshall Royal Humidor* sold in 2018 for $185,000 — a box of burl maple root reinforced with titanium, holding a library of 250 cigars.

posts.titleen: The vitola — anatomy of a format
posts.excerptsr: *Vitola* znači format cigare. Definišu je dva broja: **dužina u inčima** i **ring** (prečnik u 64-tinama inča). *Robusto* (5×50), *Toro* (6×52), *Churchill* (7×47) — svaka vitola gradi sopstveni profil dima.
posts.excerpten: *Vitola* means cigar format. It's defined by two numbers: **length in inches** and **ring** (diameter in 64ths of an inch). *Robusto* (5×50), *Toro* (6×52), *Churchill* (7×47) — each vitola builds its own smoke profile.
posts.bodysr:
Najpopularnije vitole danas:

- **Robusto** (5 × 50) — kubanski standard, 35-45 minuta dima, kompaktna i intenzivna
- **Toro** (6 × 52) — američki favorit, balans dužine i ringa
- **Churchill** (7 × 47) — duža, lakša, do 90 minuta
- **Lonsdale** (6,5 × 42) — vitka, elegantna, klasična
- **Corona** (5,5 × 42) — istorijski standard
- **Pyramide** (figurada, 6 × 52→40) — koniska forma, koncentruje arome ka glavi

Duži ring (debelja cigara) znači više duvana, hladniji dim, kompleksnije arome — ali i veće zadržavanje ulja. Kraći ring (vitka) gori brže, intenzivnije i toplo — fokus na omotač.

Figuradas — *Pyramide*, *Belicoso*, *Salomon*, *Perfecto*, *Diadema*, *Culebra* (tri uvijene cigare) — najteže su za motanje. Samo nekoliko desetina majstora na svetu može da rola pravu *Diademas*. Cena im je 3-5x viša od standardnih *Robustos*.
posts.bodyen:
The most popular vitolas today:

- **Robusto** (5 × 50) — Cuban standard, 35-45 min smoke, compact and intense
- **Toro** (6 × 52) — American favourite, balance of length and ring
- **Churchill** (7 × 47) — longer, lighter, up to 90 min
- **Lonsdale** (6.5 × 42) — slim, elegant, classic
- **Corona** (5.5 × 42) — historic standard
- **Pyramide** (figurado, 6 × 52→40) — conical form, concentrates aromas at the head

A longer ring (thicker cigar) means more tobacco, cooler smoke, more complex aromas — but more oil retention. A shorter ring (slim) burns faster, more intensely and hotter — wrapper-focused.

Figurados — *Pyramide*, *Belicoso*, *Salomon*, *Perfecto*, *Diadema*, *Culebra* (three twisted cigars) — are the hardest to roll. Only a few dozen masters in the world can roll a true *Diademas*. They cost 3-5x more than standard *Robustos*.

posts.titleen: Vuelta Abajo — the golden land of tobacco
posts.excerptsr: U zapadnom delu Kube, u provinciji Pinar del Río, prostire se 100 kilometara crvene zemlje koju zovu *Vuelta Abajo* — *Donja regija*. Ovde, na samo 25.000 hektara, raste duvan koji se smatra najboljim na planeti.
posts.excerpten: In western Cuba, in Pinar del Río province, stretches 100 kilometres of red soil they call *Vuelta Abajo* — *the Lower Region*. Here, on just 25,000 hectares, grows tobacco considered the world's finest.
posts.bodysr:
Klima Vuelta Abajo je jedinstvena: prosečna temperatura 24°C, vlažnost 75-80%, padavine 1300 mm godišnje, i posebne magle koje pre zore filtriraju sunce. Tlo je kombinacija kalcijuma, kalijuma i magnezijuma — što daje listu meku teksturu i kompleksan profil ukusa.

Najprestižniji vegasi (plantaže) — *Hoyo de Monterrey*, *Robaina*, *Cuchillas de Barbacoa* — nasleđuju se generacijama. Alejandro Robaina (1919-2010) bio je legendarni *veguero*; njegova porodica još uvek motra polja iste sortе koja je Castro odobrio za nacionalnu Cohiba liniju.

*Tabacalera Cubana* (državni monopol) gradi blendove iz tri Vuelta Abajo komponente:
- **Volado** (donji listovi) — nikotin nizak, gori dobro
- **Seco** (srednji) — aromatski balans
- **Ligero** (gornji) — najjača nota, nikotin visok, definiše snagu

Mešavinom *ligero/seco/volado* u različitim odnosima blender stvara cigaru *light*, *medium* ili *full body* karaktera. Vuelta Abajo zemlja je toliko važna da je 1999. UNESCO dodelio status *Geografske oznake porekla* za sve cigare iz ove regije.
posts.bodyen:
The Vuelta Abajo climate is unique: average 24°C, 75-80% humidity, 1300 mm of annual rainfall, and special pre-dawn fogs that filter the sun. The soil is a mix of calcium, potassium and magnesium — giving the leaf soft texture and a complex flavour profile.

The most prestigious vegas (plantations) — *Hoyo de Monterrey*, *Robaina*, *Cuchillas de Barbacoa* — pass down through generations. Alejandro Robaina (1919-2010) was a legendary *veguero*; his family still tends fields of the same variety Castro approved for the national Cohiba line.

*Tabacalera Cubana* (the state monopoly) builds blends from three Vuelta Abajo components:
- **Volado** (lower leaves) — low nicotine, burns well
- **Seco** (middle) — aromatic balance
- **Ligero** (upper) — strongest note, high nicotine, defines strength

By mixing *ligero/seco/volado* in varying ratios, the blender creates a cigar of *light*, *medium* or *full body* character. The Vuelta Abajo land is so important that in 1999 UNESCO granted it *Protected Geographical Indication* status for all cigars from the region.

posts.excerptsr: Od trenutka kada se seme posadi do trenutka kada cigara stigne u humidor prodavca, prođe minimum **18 meseci** i tačno **47 odvojenih koraka**, mahom ručnih. Svaka cigara je rezultat 47 ljudskih dodira.
posts.excerpten: From the moment the seed is planted to when the cigar reaches the retailer's humidor, a minimum of **18 months** and exactly **47 separate steps** pass — mostly by hand. Every cigar is the result of 47 human touches.
posts.bodysr:
**Polje (sept-feb):** seme se sadi septembra, biljke se stite od sunca *cheesecloth* tkaninom kod sortе *shade-grown*, beru se listovi *priming*-om — od dna ka vrhu, jedan po jedan, svake nedelje.

**Sušenje (90 dana):** listovi vise u *casas de tabaco* — drvenim kućama bez prozora — gde se temperatura i vlažnost menjaju prirodno. Klorofil se razgrađuje, list dobija žutu pa zlatno-mrku boju.

**Fermentacija (3-6 meseci, dva ciklusa):** listovi se slažu u *pilones* — visoke gomile koje dostižu 50°C iznutra. Rotiraju se ručno svake nedelje. Ovde se uklanjaju amonijak, smola, gorčina; razvija se aroma.

**Pakiranje i odležavanje (do 2 godine):** sortirano po veličini, boji i teksturi. List odleži pre nego što se mota.

**Motanje:** torcedor uzima 3-5 *filler* listova, jedan *binder*, jedan *wrapper*. Ručno mota, prešom oblikuje glavu. Brzina: dobar majstor mota 100-150 cigara dnevno.

**Kontrola:** *escogedor* sortira po nijansama (na kubanskom *Partagás*-u postoji 65 različitih shade kategorija!). *Catador* (degustator) testira slučajne primerke.

**Odležavanje u kutiji:** minimum 6 meseci, ali premium cigare često 2-5 godina pre puštanja u prodaju.

Ukupno: 47 koraka, oko 200 ljudi koji su dotakli list pre nego što ti zapališ dim.
posts.bodyen:
**Field (Sept-Feb):** seeds are planted in September, plants are protected from sun by *cheesecloth* for *shade-grown* varieties, leaves are *primed* — from bottom to top, one at a time, every week.

**Drying (90 days):** leaves hang in *casas de tabaco* — wooden houses without windows — where temperature and humidity change naturally. Chlorophyll breaks down, the leaf turns yellow then golden brown.

**Fermentation (3-6 months, two cycles):** leaves stack in *pilones* — tall piles that reach 50°C internally. Rotated by hand every week. Ammonia, resin, bitterness are removed; aroma develops.

**Packing and aging (up to 2 years):** sorted by size, colour and texture. Leaves rest before rolling.

**Rolling:** the torcedor takes 3-5 *filler* leaves, one *binder*, one *wrapper*. Rolls by hand, presses the head into shape. Speed: a good master rolls 100-150 cigars daily.

**Control:** the *escogedor* sorts by shade (Cuba's *Partagás* has 65 distinct shade categories!). A *catador* (taster) tests random samples.

**Box-aging:** minimum 6 months, but premium cigars often 2-5 years before release.

Total: 47 steps, around 200 people who touched the leaf before you light the smoke.

posts.titlesr: Ritual paljenja — kako se pali pravilno
posts.titleen: The lighting ritual — how to light properly
posts.excerptsr: Cigara se ne pali kao cigareta. Pre nego što povučeš prvi dim, prošlo je tri minuta tihe pripreme: rez 2 mm od kape, predgrejavanje cedrovom šibicom, paljenje pod uglom od 45 stepeni.
posts.excerpten: A cigar isn't lit like a cigarette. Before you draw the first smoke, three minutes of quiet preparation pass: a 2 mm cut from the cap, pre-warming with a cedar match, lighting at a 45-degree angle.
posts.bodysr:
**1. Rez (corte):** koristi giljotinu ili V-cut. Glavu (zatvoreni kraj) preseci 2 mm iznad *cap line* — vidne linije gde se kapa spaja sa wrapper-om. Punch *bušilica* je opcija za vitole sa ringom 50+.

**2. Predgrejavanje (toasting):** drži cigaru horizontalno, plamen na 1-2 cm ispod nogeza (otvoreni kraj). NE dodirivaj. Rotiraj cigaru sporim pokretima. Cilj: ravnomerno zagrejana površina pre nego što plamen dotakne duvan.

**3. Paljenje:** stavi cigaru u usta, rotiraj kroz plamen pod 45°. Lagano povlači dim — ne zatvarajući usne potpuno. Posle 5-7 sekundi izduvni dim na *foot* (otvoren kraj) da vidiš da li je gorenje ravnomerno.

**4. Prvi dim:** ne udiši! Dim se zadržava u ustima 3-5 sekundi, kruži, uživa u aromi, pa izduva. Cigara nije nikotin — to je aroma.

**5. Tempo:** jedan dim u minuti. Brže — žar se pregreva, gore se ulja, javlja se gorčina. Sporije — gasi se. Ako se ugasi, normalno; ostruži pepeo i zapali ponovo.

**Šta se ne radi:** ne palite Zippo benzinerom (kontaminira aromu), ne udišete dim, ne palite cigaretu cigarom *(uvreda majstoru)*. Dobra cigara traje 45-90 minuta — to je vreme koje sebi poklanjaš.
posts.bodyen:
**1. Cut (corte):** use a guillotine or V-cut. Cut the head (closed end) 2 mm above the *cap line* — the visible line where the cap meets the wrapper. A punch is an option for vitolas with ring 50+.

**2. Toasting:** hold the cigar horizontal, flame 1-2 cm below the foot (open end). DO NOT touch. Rotate the cigar slowly. Goal: an evenly warmed surface before the flame meets tobacco.

**3. Lighting:** put the cigar in your mouth, rotate through the flame at 45°. Draw lightly — without fully closing your lips. After 5-7 seconds blow on the foot to check the burn is even.

**4. First draw:** don't inhale! Smoke stays in the mouth 3-5 seconds, circulates, tastes the aroma, then exhales. A cigar isn't nicotine — it's aroma.

**5. Tempo:** one draw per minute. Faster — the ember overheats, oils burn, bitterness appears. Slower — it goes out. If it does, that's normal; scrape the ash and re-light.

**What not to do:** don't light with a Zippo benzine lighter (contaminates aroma), don't inhale, don't light a cigarette with a cigar *(an insult to the maker)*. A good cigar lasts 45-90 minutes — that's the time you gift yourself.
